name: colibri
description: Run GLM-5.2 (744B MoE) on a 25GB-RAM consumer machine — pure C, zero deps, experts streamed from disk. Tiny engine, immense model. 🐦
readme_summary: Colibrì is a C-based runtime for large Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models like GLM-5.2 (744B parameters) that allows them to run on consumer hardware with limited RAM by intelligently streaming model experts from disk. It manages a memory hierarchy of VRAM, RAM, and storage to maintain model quality and performance.
primary_use_case: Enabling large, parameter-heavy AI models to run on standard consumer hardware by optimizing memory management and leveraging disk storage.
cover_image_prompt: A tiny, intricate clockwork hummingbird (representing Colibrì) effortlessly lifting a massive, shimmering cloud of data (representing the 744B GLM-5.2 model) from a dusty bookshelf (representing disk storage) into a small, glowing workspace (representing consumer RAM/CPU). The hummingbird's wings are made of code snippets, and small digital particles representing streamed model experts flow from the bookshelf to the workspace. The background is a cozy, dimly lit room, emphasizing the accessibility of powerful AI on modest hardware. The overall mood is one of surprising power and efficiency. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual storytelling and a warm color palette.

name: builder
description: Craft beautiful websites effortlessly with an intuitive visual builder and publish them instantly
readme_summary: Frappe Builder is a low-code website builder that allows users to create beautiful, responsive websites with an intuitive visual interface and AI-powered generation. It emphasizes performance, offers built-in CMS capabilities, advanced scripting for customization, and a one-click publishing feature for a complete end-to-end website creation experience.
primary_use_case: Building and publishing websites quickly and easily using a visual, low-code interface.
cover_image_prompt: A digital artist's studio where a user is effortlessly sculpting a website from a block of digital clay using a Figma-like interface. The clay smoothly transforms into a beautiful, responsive website displayed on a floating screen. On one side, a prompt box shows text input for AI generation, and on the other, a 'Publish' button glows, ready to send the creation to a stylized, miniature cityscape. Subtle code snippets and data particles float around the scene, connecting the creative process to technology. The overall mood is one of ease and speed, with clean lines and a bright, modern aesthetic. 3D isometric illustration style.

name: chirp
readme_summary: Chirp is a TypeScript application that enables data transfer through sound. It encodes text into audio frequencies for transmission via speakers and decodes received audio back into text, featuring real-time frequency visualization.
key_features: Real-time frequency visualization of audio input
Send messages by converting text to sound
Receive messages by listening to sound and decoding it back to text
Distinctive start and end signatures to mark transmissions
primary_use_case: Demonstrating and experimenting with data transmission using audible sound frequencies.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical scene depicting sound waves as colorful, tangible ribbons flowing from a vintage speaker towards a stylized microphone. The ribbons are intricately woven with binary code and musical notes, representing data being transmitted. On one side, a hand types on a simple keyboard, with the text transforming into these sound ribbons. On the other side, the microphone captures the ribbons and transforms them back into legible text on a small, glowing screen. The overall mood is playful and innovative, set against a clean, minimalist background with subtle digital particle effects. The art style should be a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing clarity and a touch of magic in everyday technology.

name: pasteguard
description: AI gets the context. Not your private data. Local-first privacy proxy for browser chat, AI APIs, and coding agents.
readme_summary: PasteGuard is a local-first privacy proxy that masks Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and sensitive data before it's sent to AI models and coding agents. It acts as a protective layer, ensuring that private data remains local while still allowing AI to process contextual information.
key_features: Masks PII and secrets before reaching AI providers (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Codex, etc.)
API integration for applications and SDKs
Supports coding agents by masking sensitive context
primary_use_case: To protect sensitive user data and secrets when interacting with AI models and coding agents, by masking this information locally before it is sent to external services.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant digital librarian stands at a grand entrance to a library of knowledge, holding a glowing 'masking' tool. Before the librarian, stacks of sensitive documents (contracts, code snippets, personal notes) are being fed into a portal. The librarian uses the tool to instantly replace PII and secrets within these documents with abstract, colorful placeholder symbols. The masked documents then pass through the portal to an AI entity represented by a friendly, abstract cloud. The scene is clean and modern, with subtle UI elements showing the masking process and the repository name 'PasteGuard' subtly integrated into the library's architecture. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a bright, trustworthy color palette.
